title: plantago major var. brachyphylla
intro: Plantago major var. brachyphylla, also known as common plantain, is a perennial herb belonging to the Plantaginaceae family, characterized by its small leaves and compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of plantain are said to have several health benefits, including reducing swelling, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, plantain is often used as a tincture, tea, or salve, and can be applied topically or consumed internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
